# UC-17.1.32 · Cisco ISE License Consumption, Endpoint Capacity and MnT Log Retention

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Like a phone plan with a fixed number of devices, the access-control system can only watch a certain number of devices before the plan runs out. We track how many are in use, warn when it's nearly full, and watch how long old activity records are kept so the records don't quietly disappear sooner than agreed.*

---

## Description

Detects ISE Smart Licensing capacity exceeding 70/80/95% of the entitled count, license expiry within 90/60/30 days, Smart Licensing transport disconnection from cisco.com (which after the 90-day grace period downgrades the deployment to Essentials), and MnT operational-data retention falling below the configured target. Operational and capacity-planning detection — not an immediate-page-on-fire alert (those are different UCs), but the input that feeds the quarterly capacity-planning review.

## Value

ISE Smart Licensing has a hard cap. When consumption hits 100% of entitlement, *new endpoint authentications stop being licensed* — and depending on the deployment policy, the endpoint either auths but is logged as license-violation or is denied outright. Either way it's a P0 the moment it crosses. This UC catches the trend at 70%, 80%, 95% — giving 30+ days to procure the additional licenses. MnT retention shrinking below configured target means audit log retention is silently degrading; an auditor asking 'show me 90 days of authentication history' may be told the system has only 47.

## Implementation

Set up the Smart Licensing transport (cisco.com or on-prem CSSM satellite) on the Primary PAN — without it, this UC can collect license syslog signals but the OpenAPI `/license/system` returns stale data. Deploy the OpenAPI poller (same approach as UC-17.1.31). Schedule the search every 4h, search window `-12h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `tier`+`status` for 24h.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

On a lab ISE running Smart Licensing in EVAL state, simulate consumption climbing past 80% by configuring a large endpoint identity group and assigning Premier authorization profiles to many endpoints. The next 1h-cadence license-poller run shows consumption_pct >= 80, and UC-17.1.32 emits a row with `status=HIGH`.

### Negative scenario

Reduce consumption back below 70% by removing the Premier policy assignments. Within 1h the next poller run shows consumption_pct < 70 and UC-17.1.32 does NOT fire.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE 3.0+ Smart Licensing configured (Administration > System > Licensing) — either direct to cisco.com over the internet, or to an on-prem CSSM Satellite. Deployments running on the legacy traditional licensing model (ISE 2.x) need a different feed; this UC targets Smart Licensing.
- ERS / OpenAPI service account with `Read-only Admin` (UC-17.1.31).
- `ise_deployment.csv` lookup includes an `environment` column (lab/staging/prod).
- Splunk role `srchIndexesAllowed = nac`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(a) Syslog signals — *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO carries the 520xx license codes. *System Statistics* at WARN carries the 60530/60531/60532 MnT-retention codes. Confirm with:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (52070, 52071, 52072, 52073, 52074, 60530, 60531, 60532) earliest=-30d
| stats count by MessageCode
```

(b) OpenAPI license poller. Implement as a Splunk_TA_REST stanza or Python cron script:
```ini
[rest://ise_license_summary]
endpoint = https://<ise-pan>:443/api/v1/license/system
auth_type = basic
username = ers_readonly
password = ${SECRET}
polling_interval = 3600
response_type = json
index = nac
sourcetype = cisco:ise:license
```
The API returns a JSON object with the consumption snapshot. Normalise per tier into an event:
```
{
  "tier": "Premier",
  "entitled_count": 25000,
  "consumed_count": 18412,
  "expiry": "2026-12-31T23:59:59",
  "smart_account": "YourCo - Production",
  "transport_state": "CONNECTED"
}
```

(c) MnT retention poller. Same pattern, every 6h:
```ini
[rest://ise_mnt_retention]
endpoint = https://<ise-pan>:443/api/v1/operations/data-purge-status
polling_interval = 21600
sourcetype = cisco:ise:mnt_retention
```
Normalise into events with `db_size_gb`, `oldest_event`, `retention_days_actual`, `retention_days_configured`.

Validate:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:license" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(consumed_count) as consumed by tier
```
Expected: 1 row per active tier (Essentials and/or Advantage and/or Premier).

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Primary search:

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:license")
| stats latest(consumed_count) as consumed, latest(entitled_count) as entitled, latest(expiry) as expiry, latest(transport_state) as transport_state, latest(_time) as last_seen by tier
| eval consumption_pct = round((consumed / entitled) * 100, 1)
| eval days_to_expiry = round((strptime(expiry, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S") - now()) / 86400, 0)
| eval status = case(
    transport_state=="DISCONNECTED", "CRITICAL_DISCONNECTED",
    consumption_pct >= 95 OR days_to_expiry < 30, "CRITICAL",
    consumption_pct >= 80 OR days_to_expiry < 60, "HIGH",
    consumption_pct >= 70 OR days_to_expiry < 90, "MEDIUM",
    true(), "OK")
| where status != "OK"
| sort - consumption_pct
```

Secondary MnT retention search:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:mnt_retention" earliest=-14d
| stats avg(retention_days_actual) as avg_actual latest(retention_days_configured) as configured by host
| where avg_actual < configured * 0.85
```

Schedule both: every 4h, time range `-12h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) Compare to the PAN UI's Licensing page (Administration > System > Licensing > Smart Licensing). Numbers must agree.

(b) Confirm trend logic:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:license" earliest=-30d@d
| timechart span=1d max(consumed_count) by tier
```
The series should be approximately flat for stable deployments; growth signals onboarding, increased rollout, or possibly a new licensing model change at Cisco.

(c) Validate MnT retention:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:mnt_retention" earliest=-30d
| timechart span=1d avg(retention_days_actual) avg(retention_days_configured)
```
The two series should track each other within ~10%.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — License & Capacity Planning":
- Single value tiles per tier: consumed / entitled, % consumed, days to expiry. Colour-coded.
- 90d trend timechart of consumed per tier with horizontal entitled-count line.
- Smart-Licensing transport-state indicator.
- MnT retention panel: actual vs configured, 30d.
- Capacity projection: linear regression of consumed_count last 90d, plotted forward 90d, dashed line. Reveals when current trend hits entitled.

Quarterly review (cadence: quarterly, owner: ISE platform team + procurement):
- Review consumption trend per tier.
- Project to next renewal cycle. Procure additional entitlement if projected to exceed 90% before next renewal.

Runbook (owner: ISE platform team):
1. CRITICAL_DISCONNECTED: SSH to PAN, `show license summary`, `show license technical-support`. If transport is broken, run `license smart renew ID` and `license smart renew auth`.
2. CRITICAL (>95% consumed): emergency procurement engagement with Cisco partner. Short-term, identify the largest endpoint group consuming Premier licenses and consider downgrading to Advantage if feature requirements allow.
3. HIGH (>80% consumed): notify procurement. Check the consumption trend — is this expected onboarding or a misconfiguration?
4. MnT retention shrinking: investigate the cause. Common causes — increased event volume per session due to new logging categories enabled; bulk endpoint storm; misconfigured retention setting. Adjust retention or scale MnT.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **License poller returns stale data** — Smart Licensing transport is broken. Force a sync from the PAN CLI: `license smart renew ID`. The next poll cycle should show fresh data.

- **`consumed_count > entitled_count`** — Hard cap exceeded. New endpoint sessions may already be denied (depending on deployment policy). This is a P0 — engage procurement immediately and consider emergency policy redistribution.

- **Tier shows in OpenAPI but not in syslog** — Logging Categories don't include the *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO. Switch on; license events appear within 1 day.

- **MnT retention dropped from configured 90 days to 47 days** — MnT is event-volume saturated. Either expand MnT capacity (add a second MnT, switch to a larger appliance), reduce logging-category verbosity, or reduce configured retention.

- **Eval license alerts firing on Day 1** — `transport_state=EVAL` is not a real disconnection. Add a 90-day grace exclusion for new deployments to the alert filter.

- **Capacity projection panel shows projected breach in 30 days but no trend over 90 days** — Insufficient lookback for the projection. Adjust to 60 days, accept higher uncertainty, or wait until 90 days of data accumulates.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:license")
| stats latest(consumed_count) as consumed, latest(entitled_count) as entitled, latest(expiry) as expiry, latest(transport_state) as transport_state by tier
| eval consumption_pct = round((consumed / entitled) * 100, 1)
| eval days_to_expiry = round((strptime(expiry, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S") - now()) / 86400, 0)
| eval status = case(
    transport_state=="DISCONNECTED", "CRITICAL_DISCONNECTED",
    consumption_pct >= 95 OR days_to_expiry < 30, "CRITICAL",
    consumption_pct >= 80 OR days_to_expiry < 60, "HIGH",
    consumption_pct >= 70 OR days_to_expiry < 90, "MEDIUM",
    true(), "OK")
| where status != "OK"
| sort - consumption_pct
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value tiles per tier (Essentials/Advantage/Premier): consumed/entitled, %% consumed, days to expiry. Colour-coded by status thresholds. (2) Trend timechart: consumed_count per tier last 90d; overlay entitled_count as a horizontal line — the visual gap is your headroom. (3) MnT retention panel: actual retention vs configured retention, last 30d. (4) Smart-Licensing transport-state status indicator (green=CONNECTED, red=DISCONNECTED, yellow=EVAL).

## Known False Positives

- **Eval license** — Fresh deployments run 90-day evaluation licenses. The `transport_state` shows `EVAL` and `expiry` is the eval end date — this is not a CRITICAL_DISCONNECTED issue, it's expected. Add `transport_state != "EVAL"` to the filter for the first 90 days post-deployment.
- **MnT retention shrinks during a known endpoint storm** — A massive auth event (e.g. 100k endpoints reauthenticating after a CRL change) inflates MnT log volume, briefly compressing retention days. If retention rebounds within 7d, do not page; only alert when retention is < configured for >14 consecutive days.
- **Smart Licensing transport flap during ISP maintenance** — A single DISCONNECTED event followed by CONNECTED within 1h is normal. Only alert when DISCONNECTED persists across 3+ polls (= 3h).
- **Premier-tier consumption near 100% in TrustSec-only deployments** — Some deployments deliberately keep Premier consumption right at the limit because the TrustSec/SGT feature is the only Premier consumer and they've sized exactly. The trend should be flat — if consumed_count is climbing, it's a misconfiguration in policy assignment.

- [Cisco ISE 3.x Smart Licensing — Smart Account / Virtual Account setup](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/collateral/security/identity-services-engine/at-a-glance-c45-744388.html)
